heading: Eligibility for consideration for promotion: maximum years of service in grade
label: 14304 Eligibility for consideration for promotion: maximum years of service in grade
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1691(b)(1) of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title

Minimum Service Qualifications for Promotion, , , provided that during the five-year period beginning on , the Secretary of the Army and the Secretary of the Air Force could waive the provisions of this section and during any period in which such a waiver is in effect, establish minimum periods of total years of commissioned service an officer must have served to be eligible for consideration for promotion to the grade of captain, major, or lieutenant colonel by boards convened under .Pub. children.heading: .—Consideration for Promotion Within Specified Times
children.label: a .—Consideration for Promotion Within Specified Times

children.text: Officers described in paragraph (3) shall be placed in the promotion zone for that officer’s grade and competitive category, and shall be considered for promotion to the next higher grade by a promotion board convened under , far enough in advance of completing the years of service in grade specified in the following table so that, if the officer is recommended for promotion, the promotion may be effective on or before the date on which the officer will complete those years of service.section 14101(a) of this titleCurrent GradeMaximum years of service in gradeFirst lieutenant or Lieutenant (junior grade)5 yearsCaptain or Navy Lieutenant7 yearsMajor or Lieutenant commander7 years
Paragraph (1) is subject to subsections (a), (b), and (c) of and applies without regard to vacancies.section 14301 of this title

Paragraph (1) applies to an officer who is on the reserve active-status list of the Army, Navy, Air Force, or Marine Corps and who holds a permanent appointment in the grade of first lieutenant, captain, or major as a reserve of the Army, Air Force, or Marine Corps, or to an officer on the reserve active-status list of the Navy in the grade of lieutenant (junior grade), lieutenant, or lieutenant commander as a reserve of the Navy, and who, while holding that appointment, has not been considered by a selection board convened under section 14101(a) or 14502 of this title for promotion to the next higher grade.

children.heading: .—Promotion Date
children.label: b .—Promotion Date
children.text: An officer holding a permanent grade specified in the table in subsection (a) who is recommended for promotion to the next higher grade by a selection board the first time the officer is considered for promotion while in or above the promotion zone and who is placed on an approved promotion list established under shall (if not promoted sooner or removed from that list by the President or by reason of declination) be promoted, without regard to the existence of a vacancy, on the date on which the officer completes the maximum years of service in grade specified in subsection (a). The preceding sentence is subject to the limitations of .section 14308(a) of this titlesection 12011 of this title

children.heading: .—Waiver Authority for Navy and Marine Corps Running Mate System
children.label: c .—Waiver Authority for Navy and Marine Corps Running Mate System
children.text: If the Secretary of the Navy establishes promotion zones for officers on the reserve active-status list of the Navy or the Marine Corps Reserve in accordance with a running mate system under , the Secretary may waive the requirements of subsection (a) to the extent the Secretary considers necessary in any case in which the years of service for promotion, or for consideration for promotion, within those zones will exceed the maximum years of service in grade specified in subsection (a).section 14306 of this title
